ogTitle: Laceby vs Laen East: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Laceby and Laen East.
heading: Laceby vs Laen East — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Laceby, Victoria and Laen East, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Laceby has a lower overall climate risk score (29/100) compared to Laen East (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Laceby or Laen East safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Laceby has a flood risk score of 27/100, while Laen East scores 58/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Laceby scores 18/100 for bushfire risk. Laen East scores 28/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Laceby or Laen East?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Laceby has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.95% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
